items.briefing:
The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) has issued a warning about an active, large-scale campaign targeting vulnerabilities in content management systems (CMS) — such as WordPress — used by Australian organisations. If your organisation runs a website, ensure it is patched and up to date. Separately, a recent Telstra outage has highlighted how heavily Australian businesses depend on a small number of telecommunications providers, and the risks this creates when services go down unexpectedly.

To protect against social engineering attacks — where scammers manipulate people into sharing information or access — be cautious of unexpected calls, emails, or messages requesting urgent action. **Tip:** If you manage a website, check with your IT team or provider today that your CMS software and plugins are fully updated.

featured.title: Telstra outage exposes Australia's reliance on carriers
featured.quote: National carrier outage exposed critical infrastructure vulnerability affecting emergency services across Australia.
